proofs.proof_tex: Proof. Fix theta=(pbar,V_1,V_3,V_4) in Theta with 0<=pbar<=1. The certified constructive content follows directly from the frozen dependency spine. First, thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorithm applies to theta. If pbar is 0 or 1, the admissible class N(pbar) is the singleton {delta_pbar}, so the algorithm outputs delta_pbar and the optimality certificate is the direct singleton certificate. If 0<pbar<1, prop:support-at-most-three reduces the global minimization of V over N(pbar) to the finite search over optimizers supported on at most three centered points. The construction named in def:branch-map-contact-handle organizes those support patterns into the semialgebraic S1/S2/S3 contact programs. The constructive theorem then solves these finitely many polynomial programs by exact real-algebraic elimination, CAD/Thom encodings, root isolation, and exact comparison of algebraic V-values, discards infeasible candidates, and returns a law nu_alg in N(pbar) with V(nu_alg)<=V(nu) for every nu in N(pbar). Its certificate is also exact: boundary and degenerate branches are certified by singleton feasibility or exact candidate comparison, while interior contact branches are certified by constructing the quartic residual Q, checking Q>=0 on D_pbar, and checking the outer inequality H(s)>=V(nu_alg) on [0,pbar(1-pbar)]; by thm:global-certificate this verifies global optimality. Second, lem:explicit-profile-value supplies the closed-form outer profile used in that certificate. With p=pbar, q=1-p, C=pq, and A=q-p, it gives F(0)=0 and, for s in [0,C], F(s)=min{min_{t in I_2(s)} Phi_2(s,t), min_{r in I_3(s)} Phi_3(s,r)}, with I_2, I_3, Phi_2, and Phi_3 as stated there. Therefore H(s)=V_0+F(s)-V_4s^2 is an explicit piecewise-algebraic function. Third, prop:explicit-two-point-cell gives the complete closed-form singleton/two-point optimizer: the value above V_0 is the minimum of 0, g_L(t)=p(p+t)(V_1+V_3t+V_4t^2) on [-p,A], and g_R(t)=q(q-t)(V_1+V_3t+V_4t^2) on [A,q], with the full finite candidate list supplied by endpoints and the two displayed stationary equations. The constructive algorithm performs the exact algebraic comparison between those closed-form singleton/two-point candidates and the three-point endpoint-interior candidates. Hence the delivered content is fully proved: over all Theta there is an exact terminating optimizer and certificate, H is closed form, and the singleton/two-point class has a closed-form optimizer. The only residual question is not existence, optimality, or computability, but atlas compression: whether the exact CAD/Thom branch output for all globally active three-point cells can be rewritten as compact human-readable inequalities and closed-form formulas.

open_obligations.what_is_open: Simplify the exact CAD/Thom branch output into a compact human-readable region-by-region symbolic atlas for every globally active three-point support cell, including interpretable inequalities and closed-form formulas for each cell.
open_obligations.obstruction: The constructive algorithm proves existence, computability, and exact certification by solving the finite semialgebraic branch programs, but CAD cell decompositions and Thom encodings need not collapse to short symbolic inequalities. No frozen primitive, established theorem, or available closed-form branch calculation supplies a compact printed atlas for the full three-point region of the quartic coefficient space.
open_obligations.attempted: The proof uses the available complete route: support-at-most-three reduction, the explicit fixed-second-moment profile H(s), exact singleton/two-point endpoint reduction, and semialgebraic CAD/Thom optimization and certification. These close the design-construction theorem but stop short of symbolic simplification of every three-point cell.
open_obligations.partial_result: For every theta=(pbar,V_1,V_3,V_4) with 0<=pbar<=1, thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorithm returns a globally optimal saturation law supported on at most three points and certifies it. The profile H(s) is explicit by lem:explicit-profile-value, and the singleton/two-point cells are closed form by prop:explicit-two-point-cell.
